author: y4rr
content: Install succeeded — sudo dnf install -y cuda-nvcc-13-1 (391 MiB) gave us /usr/local/cuda-13.1/bin/ptxas. Ran ptxas --gpu-name=sm_86 on the extracted REDUCE_SUM_PTX and got the specific error in seconds: 'Unknown video selector: .x' on the line 'mov.u32 %tid, %tid.x;'. The bug was a register-name collision: user-declared %tid clashes with PTX's special %tid.x/y/z registers. The assembler parses %tid.x ambiguously when there's a user register named %tid in scope. Working dot-kernel uses %r_tid (line 4269) precisely to avoid this. Diagnosed in under 60 seconds with ptxas — proves the install was the right call. ALL 12 affected kernels (the 6 reductions plus embed_lookup_batch, scatter_add_rows, batchnorm_forward, maxpool2d_forward, avgpool2d_forward, fused_gru_forward) had the same bug. Fixed via 2 file-wide replace_all edits (%tid, → %my_tid, and %tid; → %my_tid;). Now every reduction kernel ptxas-validates clean. cargo test PASSES across all binaries (was 27 failing before, now 0). Workspace builds clean.
